prompts.abstract: A cylindrical object with a flat circular top and bottom, smooth surface, and uniform diameter.
prompts.description: A cylindrical object with a flat circular top and bottom, smooth surface, and uniform diameter.
prompts.beginner: A cylindrical shape is created with a height of zero point seven five units and a diameter of zero point four eight nine one units. The cylinder is formed by transforming and extruding a circular sketch.
prompts.intermediate: To create a cylindrical object, start by setting up a new coordinate system with specific angles and translations. Then, draw a circular shape on the first face. This circle is scaled down and transformed into a three-dimensional form. The transformed shape is then extruded to create a solid cylinder. The final dimensions of the cylinder are a height of zero point seven five units, and both the length and width are zero point four eight nine one units. The cylinder is created through a series of precise transformations and extrusions, resulting in a well-defined solid body.
prompts.expert: Create a cylindrical object by following these detailed steps. First, set up a new coordinate system with Euler angles of zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and a translation vector of zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, create a single loop by drawing a circle centered at coordinates zero point two four four six, zero point two four four six with a radius of zero point two four four six. Scale the entire two-dimensional sketch by a factor of zero point four eight nine one. Transform this scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it with the same vector used for the coordinate system. Extrude the transformed sketch zero point seven five units along the normal direction, ensuring that no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Perform a boolean operation to create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the cylindrical object will be a length of zero point four eight nine one three zero four three four seven eight two six zero eight six five units, a width of zero point four eight nine one three zero four three four seven eight two six zero eight six five units, and a height of zero point seven five units.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a cylindrical object by following these detailed steps. First, set up a new coordinate system with Euler angles of zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, and a translation vector of zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In this sketch, create a single loop by drawing a circle centered at coordinates zero point two four four six, zero point two four four six with a radius of zero point two four four six. Scale the entire two-dimensional sketch by a factor of zero point four eight nine one. Transform this scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it with the same vector used for the coordinate system. Extrude the transformed sketch zero point seven five units along the normal direction, ensuring that no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Perform a boolean operation to create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The final dimensions of the cylindrical object will be a length of zero point four eight nine one three zero four three four seven eight two six zero eight six five units, a width of zero point four eight nine one three zero four three four seven eight two six zero eight six five units, and a height of zero point seven five units.
</level1>

<level2>
To create a cylindrical object, start by setting up a new coordinate system with specific angles and translations. Then, draw a circular shape on the first face. This circle is scaled down and transformed into a three-dimensional form. The transformed shape is then extruded to create a solid cylinder. The final dimensions of the cylinder are a height of zero point seven five units, and both the length and width are zero point four eight nine one units. The cylinder is created through a series of precise transformations and extrusions, resulting in a well-defined solid body.
</level2>

<level3>
A cylindrical shape is created with a height of zero point seven five units and a diameter of zero point four eight nine one units. The cylinder is formed by transforming and extruding a circular sketch.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Cylindrical Object

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to [0.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Set the Translation Vector to [0.0, 0.75, 0.0].

#### Draw the 2D Sketch
- **Face 1**
  - **Loop 1**
    - Draw a circle with a center at (0.2446, 0.2446) and a radius of 0.2446.

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scaling factor of 0.4891 to the 2D sketch.

#### Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the sketch using the Euler Angles [0.0, 0.0, -90.0] degrees.
- Translate the sketch using the Translation Vector [0.0, 0.75, 0.0].

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the sketch 0.75 units along the normal direction.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.

#### Boolean Operation
- Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch.

#### Final Dimensions
- Length: 0.48913043478260865 units
- Width: 0.48913043478260865 units
- Height: 0.75 units
</part_1>
